data.title: Pangea Lab and Gene Solutions collaborate for cancer diagnostics in US. Pangea is CLIA-certified, CAP-accredited for NGS.
data.description: In Irvine, California, on January 21, 2026, Pangea Laboratory revealed a significant partnership with Gene Solutions, an international biotech company. Pangea Laboratory, recognized for their expertise in Next-Generation Sequencing and molecular diagnostic advancement, holds certifications from CLIA and CAP. This collaboration aims to combine their strengths in innovation and validation for molecular diagnostics.

data.title: commercetools launches new standalone offering, AgenticLift, to accelerate revenue without replatforming. Allows connection to AI shopping experiences without disruption.
data.description: commercetools has unveiled its inaugural standalone agentic solution, enabling enterprises to swiftly and securely join AI-driven shopping enhancements without the need for replatforming or interrupting current commerce functions. This allows companies to seamlessly integrate their in-house or outdated systems with AI-powered capabilities.
Blackrock COO: AI deals show financial engineering creativity.

data.title: Board and Microsoft collaborate to launch Board Agents on Microsoft Foundry, offering AI agents for real-world planning with Microsoft Azure.
data.description: A new development has been unveiled by Board, a prominent Enterprise Planning Platforms provider based in Boston. The company introduced Board Agents on Microsoft Foundry, featuring a collection of specialized AI agents designed to aid in practical planning choices for finance, supply chain, and merchandising. This collaboration marks the debut of a Board enterprise planning tool created using Microsoft Foundry, leveraging the advanced agentic AI resources of Microsoft Azure for enhanced efficiency.

data.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticizes U.S. defense contractors for delays and high executive pay; contracts behind 5-7 years.
data.description: At the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent criticized major U.S. defense contractors for their underperformance. Bessent highlighted significant delays in contract fulfillment, with contracts reportedly five to seven years behind schedule. He expressed disappointment, stating that these companies have failed the American people who rely on them. Bessent called for priorities to shift towards building more factories and reducing excessive stock buybacks rather than focusing on high executive compensation. He specifically mentioned defense CEOs earning between $30-$50 million annually despite not meeting expectations. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SureWerx® launching Dickies® Safety Footwear platform in Q4 this year, featuring a range of safety-toe and soft-toe options.
data.description: SureWerx®, a prominent global supplier of safety, tool, and equipment items, has disclosed its plan to introduce Dickies® Safety Footwear, a new footwear line anticipated to be released in the fourth quarter of this year. Drawing on a rich legacy of over 100 years in workwear, the Dickies® Safety Footwear range promises uncompromised performance for the worksite. The collection will feature a diverse selection of safety-toe and soft-toe alternatives, embodying durability and reliability.

data.title: Main Post Partners acquires HomeWell Franchising Inc.®, franchisor of HomeWell Care Services, for growth collaboration.
data.description: In a recent development, Main Post Partners has successfully acquired HomeWell Franchising, the parent company of HomeWell Care Services. This acquisition marks the beginning of a strategic partnership aimed at facilitating the expansion and advancement of HomeWell Care Services.

data.title: Wildwood Trading Group names Luke Warford COO and CFO; leading lumber sales firm, founded in 2018, rapidly expanding nationwide.
data.description: Wildwood Trading Group, based in Tualatin, Oregon, has appointed Luke Warford as its Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer. In his new role, Warford is responsible for overseeing the company's financial strategy, operational execution, and market-based risk management programs as Wildwood expands its national presence. Since its establishment in 2018, Wildwood has seen substantial growth and is recognized as a rapidly expanding North American lumber sales and distribution firm. The appointment of Warford signifies the company's commitment to further advancement.

data.title: Fortune Minerals finishes cobalt sulphate testing in Canada, confirms production of high-quality product for NICO Project.
data.description: In London, Ontario, Fortune Minerals Limited disclosed the successful completion of validation test work for an enhanced hydrometallurgical flowsheet aimed at producing battery-grade cobalt sulfate heptahydrate for the NICO cobalt-gold-bismuth-copper project in Canada. The company (TSX: FT) (OTCQB: FTMDF) confirmed the ability to produce a top-notch cobalt sulfate heptahydrate product for the project. This achievement underscores significant progress towards leveraging the lucrative market for cobalt in the burgeoning battery industry. For more information, visit the company's website at www.fortuneminerals.com.

data.title: Jacobs to modernize large wastewater facility in Western US, generating renewable energy; awarded contract for upgrades at San José–Santa Clara RWF in California.
data.description: An upgraded facility in San José is set to convert waste into renewable energy. Jacobs, a design-build contractor (NYSE: J), has been chosen for the project at the San José–Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility (RWF) in California. The initiative aims to enhance waste transformation processes at the facility, contributing to renewable energy generation. The upgrades signify a step towards sustainable practices and resource utilization.

data.title: Fujifilm partners with Voicebrook to integrate voice-enabled reporting in Synapse Pathology system.
data.description: A partnership has been formed between FUJIFILM Healthcare Americas Corporation and Voicebrook, Inc. FUJIFILM is a major supplier of diagnostic imaging and enterprise imaging solutions, while Voicebrook is known for its pathology reporting solutions. The goal of this collaboration is to integrate Voicebrook's VoiceOver PRO reporting platform with Fujifilm's Synapse® Pathology, a pathology PACS. This integration aims to improve case management through advancements in image digitization. This announcement comes as FUJIFILM continues to expand its presence in the healthcare industry.

data.title: Always Best Care named No. 454 on Entrepreneur Franchise 500 list for steady growth and entrepreneur accessibility.
data.description: Always Best Care Senior Services, a prominent senior care franchise based in Rocklin, California, has been acknowledged for its consistent expansion and appeal to entrepreneurs. As of January 21, 2026, the franchise has secured the No. 454 spot on a reputable ranking list in the United States. This recognition underlines the franchise's success and accessibility, making it an attractive option for those interested in the senior care sector.

data.title: Curaleaf opens adult-use sales in its only Maine location, celebrating launch at Bangor dispensary. #Curaleaf #Maine #Cannabis
data.description: On January 21, 2026, Curaleaf Holdings, Inc., a prominent global supplier of consumer cannabis items, unveiled its sole adult-use store in Maine. The company, listed on TSX as CURA and on OTCQX as CURLF, marked this launch as a significant development.

data.title: Total Return Securities Fund's tender offer for 4 million shares expired, with over 12 million shares tendered. Accepted shares prorated at 98% NAV.
data.description: Today, the Total Return Securities Fund (NYSE: SWZ) disclosed that its tender offer, aiming to acquire up to 4 million of the Fund’s shares for cash, concluded at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on January 20, 2026. The Equiniti Trust Company, LLC reported that a total of 12,035,603.471 shares were validly offered for purchase. Since this number exceeds the target shares, the Fund will proportionally accept shares for payment at a rate of 98% of the per share net asset value at the close of today's trading session on the New York Stock Exchange.
